text: This is smartpipe. You point it at a folder of invoices, ask a question in plain English, and get structured data back.
Each answer stays linked to the file it came from.
And it isn't just text. It reads PDFs and images, and listens to audio and video natively.
Here it reads a mixed folder and pulls one plain answer out of every file.
It cuts the pile down first, so the model only reads what's left.
Every call shows up on the receipt, and reruns come straight from the cache.
You can embed a whole folder once, right on your own machine.
Then you can search the whole folder by meaning, just by describing what you're looking for.
One command turns your documents, audio, and video into a knowledge graph.
It finds the people, companies, and places in your files, and connects them.
And it's one brew install away: smartpipe, semantic pipes for your terminal.
